titleStmt.title: The Lone Woman and Last Indians Digital Archive

respStmt.orgName: University of South Carolina Provost Humanities Grants
University of South Carolina Department of English
Center for Digital Humanities at the University of South Carolina
Magellan Scholar Program at the University of South Carolina
National Society of the Daughters of the American Revolution
Carnegie-Whitney Grant from the American Library Association

availability.p: Periodicals and books published before 1 January 1923 are in the public domain. If re-publishing, quoting from, or reproducing images from this digital archive, we request that you cite it as follows: Lone Woman and Last Indians Digital Archive, Center for Digital Humanities, University of South Carolina.

Periodicals and books published on or after 1 January 1923 may be protected by U.S. Copyright Law. All attempts to secure permission for republishing post-1923 material in this archive have been made. The names, logos, and identifying images, as well as the content of the articles, remain the property of the periodicals and/or of the authors and artists themselves. Further reproduction, as well as quotation beyond fair use, is prohibited; permission must be obtained from the copyright holder. Archival material published in this archive is used with permission of the institution or library that holds it. Further reproduction, as well as quotation beyond fair use, is prohibited; permission must be obtained from the copyright holder.
Responsibility for any use of the contents of this digital archive rests exclusively with the user.

bibl.title: Charming Visitor To Be Honored This Afternoon

title.emph: Charming Visitor To Be Honored This Afternoon

div.p: In compliment to the charming Miss Nell Skaggs, who will depart on Wednesday after enjoying the fall season here, Mrs. Bryan F. Williams will entertain this afternoon with a bridge tea at her home. Guests at the event will include members of the younger social set.
Mrs. Malcom Walker of Chicago has arrived here to spend the winter and will reside at the Highland Inn.
Mrs. Walker has visited here previously.
Members of [the] George Washington Chapter, Daughters of the American Revolution, were entertained Thursday afternoon at the home of Mrs. C. A. Vedder.
Two interesting papers were read, one, "Morris Jumel House" given by Mrs. C. G. Waller, being part of the program for the year on historic buildings of America.

The other paper, read by Mrs. L. C. Van Name, was entitled "The Lone Woman of San Nicholas Island," and dealt with the story of a woman, Juana Maria, who lived for 40 years alone on the island of San Nicholas, off the coast of California, building her own shelter and making her own garments from the materials found there. She was accidentally abandoned when missionaries removed the natives from this island. Mrs. Van Name explained that the D. A. R. chapter of Santa Barbara had erected a tablet in memory of the woman.
Miss Sue Larson conducted a general discussion on national activities of the D. A. R.
Mrs[.] Vedder was assisted in entertaining by Mrs. Cortes Pauls.
The Phi Alpha Sigma Fraternity will entertain with an informal holiday gathering atthe fraternity house Wednesday evening.

Bernard Bernadoni, president of the Galveston Reserve Officers and first lieutenant of infantry reserves, will be among those going from here to attend the military reception and dance to be given tonight at River Oaks by the Houston reserve officers.

note.p: "Copyright 2013 The Galveston County Daily News. Republished with permission of The Galveston County Daily News. No further republication or redistribution is permitted without the written consent of The Daily News."
